author: bannister
text:
Gitignore + runtime-paths cover .balls/code-refs

bl-f37b added a `--resolve-remote` code-refs cache under
`.balls/code-refs/<sha1>.git`. It was not in `ensure_main_gitignore`'s
wanted list nor in `review_safety::RUNTIME_PATHS`, so a non-bare repo
that opted in once could silently commit a forge clone on the next
`git add -A`, and `bl review` had no backstop if a stale gitignore
let one through.

- `store_init::ensure_main_gitignore`: add `.balls/code-refs` to the
  non-stealth wanted list (mirrors `.balls/worktree`).
- `review_safety::RUNTIME_PATHS`: add `.balls/code-refs` so the
  post-squash backstop rejects a cache that snuck past the gitignore.
- New unit test `commit_touches_runtime_flags_code_refs_cache` covers
  the deep-file shape (`.balls/code-refs/foo.git/HEAD`).
- Touched up two adjacent docstrings/error wording to match the
  now-four-entry runtime-paths set.

No migration code: pre-bl-f37b clones already need `bl init` re-run to
pick up the gitignore line (idempotent), same story as the prior
runtime-path additions.

Parallel gap deferred: `.balls/state-repo` (bl-ffb4) has the same
shape and is also missing from both lists. Out of scope here per the
ball, worth a follow-up.
